description_long: This is a vintage wooden radio receiver with an integrated record player, featuring a hinged lid that opens to reveal the turntable. The front panel is adorned with a large, cream-colored speaker grille, two prominent tuning and volume knobs, and a horizontal frequency display with several push buttons below. The sides of the cabinet display vented panels, likely for additional speaker output or heat dissipation. The back of the unit is mostly solid wood with a perforated metal panel for ventilation and access to internal components. The wood finish is a warm, polished brown with visible grain patterns, giving it a classic mid-20th-century aesthetic. The overall construction appears robust, with a combination of wood, metal, and plastic elements.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a record player under a hinged lid, featuring a cream speaker grille, tuning knobs, and a frequency display on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open lid, record player, speaker grille, tuning knobs, and frequency display.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the vented panel and the partially open lid revealing the turntable.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the wood grain back, a long ventilation slot, and a perforated metal panel at the bottom.
description_view_3: Right side view with a vented panel and the open lid, similar to the left side.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
